controlNotes: The Soundcraft Ui16 is a browser-controlled digital rack mixer with 16 remote-controllable inputs (12 mic/line XLR combo + 2 stereo RCA pairs + 1 stereo 3.5mm). Connect your device to the Ui16's Wi-Fi network (or Ethernet) and open Chrome to the mixer's IP address (default: 192.168.1.1 / ui16.local). Each mono channel has a fully parametric 4-band EQ plus a sweepable HPF, gate, compressor, and delay. Phantom power (+48V) is per-channel in the Preamp tab. NOTE: The Ui16's USB port supports 2-channel direct-to-memory recording only — it does NOT function as a multi-channel audio interface to a DAW. To analyze individual channels in SoundPilot, use the mixer's Solo/PFL output routed to your audio interface input and solo each channel in turn.

instructionTemplates.selectChannel: In the Soundcraft Ui app, tap the CH {channel} strip at the top to select it and open its channel view
instructionTemplates.openEQ: In the channel view, tap the EQ tab to open the 4-band parametric EQ
instructionTemplates.enableHPF: In the EQ tab, tap the HPF button to enable the high-pass filter, then drag the HPF frequency knob to {frequency}Hz
instructionTemplates.setBandFrequency: Tap the {band} band, then drag the Frequency knob (or double-tap to type) to {frequency}Hz
instructionTemplates.setBandGain: Drag the {band} Gain knob {direction} to set approximately {amount}dB. The EQ curve updates in real time.
instructionTemplates.saveSettings: Tap the menu icon (top-right) and choose Save Show to save your current settings to a show file
